fields.label: Base URL
fields.description: Base URL of the query. Leave blank if you want to handle it in the query editor.

ui.placeholder: Leave blank and you can specify full URL in the query.

fields.label: Password
fields.description: password

fields.label: Bearer token
fields.description: bearer token

fields.label: Key
fields.description: api key key

fields.label: Value
fields.description: api key value

fields.label: Add to
fields.description: Add api key to header/query params.

fields.label: Access Key
fields.description: aws access key

fields.label: Secret Key
fields.description: aws secret key

fields.label: Grant Type
fields.description: This refers to OAuth2 grant type

fields.label: Scopes
fields.description: Scopes optionally specifies a list of requested permission scopes. Enter comma separated values

fields.label: OAuth2 endpoint params
fields.description: OAuth2 endpoint params

fields.label: Email
fields.description: Email is the OAuth client identifier used when communicating with the configured OAuth provider.

fields.label: Private Key Identifier
fields.description: PrivateKeyID contains an optional hint indicating which key is being used

fields.label: Private Key
fields.description: PrivateKey contains the contents of an RSA private key or the contents of a PEM file that contains a private key. The provided private key is used to sign JWT payloads

fields.label: Subject
fields.description: Subject is the optional user to impersonate.

fields.label: Custom Token Header
fields.description: Once the token retrieved, the same will be sent to subsequent request's header with the key "Authorization". If the API require different key, provide the key here. Defaults to Authorization

fields.label: Custom Token Template
fields.description: Token Template allows you to customize the token value using the template. This will be Authorization header value. String ${__oauth2.access_token} will be replaced with actual access token

fields.label: Token request headers
fields.description: OAuth2 token request headers

fields.label: Azure cloud
fields.description: Azure cloud type

fields.label: Storage account name
fields.description: Azure blob storage account name

fields.label: Storage account key
fields.description: Azure blob storage account key
Azure Blob account URL template. Not exposed in the configuration editor; the backend fills it from azureBlobCloudType on load.

fields.label: Allowed hosts
fields.description: List of allowed host names. Enter the base URL names. ex: https://example.com

fields.label: Skip TLS Verify
fields.description: Skip TLS Verify

fields.label: With CA Cert
fields.description: Needed for verifying self-signed TLS Certs

fields.label: TLS Client Auth
fields.description: TLS Client Auth

fields.label: Server Name
fields.description: Server Name

fields.label: Proxy URL
fields.description: Proxy URL. Don't set the username or password here

fields.label: Proxy User Name
fields.description: Optional: Proxy Username. This functionality should only be used with legacy web sites. RFC 2396 warns that interpreting Userinfo this way "is NOT RECOMMENDED, because the passing of authentication information in clear text (such as URI) has proven to be a security risk in almost every case where it has been used."

fields.label: Proxy Password
fields.description: Optional: Proxy Password. This functionality should only be used with legacy web sites. RFC 2396 warns that interpreting Userinfo this way "is NOT RECOMMENDED, because the passing of authentication information in clear text (such as URI) has proven to be a security risk in almost every case where it has been used."

fields.label: Query security
fields.description: Option to handle insecure query content such as sensitive headers in the dashboard query

fields.label: Ignore status code check
fields.description: When enabled, the datasource will process response body even for HTTP error status codes (4xx, 5xx). This is useful for APIs that return useful data in error responses, such as detailed error messages or partial data during service degradation.

fields.label: Allow dangerous HTTP methods
fields.description: By default Infinity only allows GET and POST HTTP methods to reduce the risk of accidental and potentially destructive payloads. If you need PUT, PATCH or DELETE methods, make use of this setting with caution. Note: Infinity does not evaluate any permissions against the underlying API

fields.label: Include cookies
fields.description: List of cookies to forward. Enter the cookie keys. ex: access_token or grafana_session_expiry

ui.placeholder: Enter the cookie names (enter key to add)

fields.label: Reference data
fields.description: Named inline datasets reusable in queries via source='reference'.

ui.placeholder: Give an unique name to your reference data
Enter data here. either json / csv / tsv / xml / html

fields.description: Named datasource-level saved queries that other queries can reference via type='global'. The individual InfinityQuery shape is defined by the query editor and is intentionally opaque at the datasource-config level.
When true, the plugin swaps in the in-memory mock client (used only by the plugin's own tests). Not exposed in the configuration editor.

fields.label: Custom HTTP Headers
fields.description: Additional HTTP headers sent with every request. Header names are stored in jsonData (httpHeaderName<N>); header values are write-only in secureJsonData (httpHeaderValue<N>).

validations.message: Header name must start with a letter and contain only letters, digits, or hyphens.

fields.label: URL Query Params
fields.description: URL query parameters appended to every request. Names are stored in jsonData (secureQueryName<N>); values are write-only in secureJsonData (secureQueryValue<N>).

relationships.description: Basic / Digest auth credentials. The editor writes basicAuthUser at the root level and basicAuthPassword under secureJsonData whenever the selected auth method is basicAuth or digestAuth.
API key credentials. apiKeyKey (name) and apiKeyValue (secret) are used together; apiKeyType picks the transport (header or query param).
AWS SigV4 credentials. All five fields go together when auth_method is 'aws'. Note that region, service, and authType live under the nested jsonData.aws object.
OAuth2 client credentials grant: client_id + client secret + token URL. All under jsonData.oauth2 except the secret.
OAuth2 JWT grant: email + private key + token URL.
Azure Blob credentials — storage account name (jsonData) and storage account key (secureJsonData). The backend derives azureBlobAccountUrl from azureBlobCloudType on load.
TLS mutual auth: enabling jsonData.tlsAuth requires serverName + client cert + client key.
Self-signed / custom CA verification: enabling jsonData.tlsAuthWithCACert requires the CA certificate PEM in secureJsonData.tlsCACert.
Custom health check: when enabled, the backend replaces the default health check with an HTTP GET against customHealthCheckUrl.

Custom outbound proxy: proxy_type='url' turns on proxy_url + optional proxy_username + optional proxyUserPassword. proxy_type='env' uses the HTTP_PROXY/HTTPS_PROXY env vars; 'none' disables all proxying.

instructions.msg: Nine authentication methods are available, selected via jsonData.auth_method: 'none' (default), 'basicAuth', 'bearerToken', 'apiKey', 'digestAuth', 'oauthPassThru' (forward the signed-in user's OAuth token), 'oauth2' (grant type selected via jsonData.oauth2.oauth2_type: 'client_credentials', 'jwt', 'others'), 'aws' (AWS SigV4), and 'azureBlob'. Configure exactly one; only the credentials of the selected method are used. Switching methods does not clear the other methods' fields or secrets — stale values are harmless but confusing.

Minimal payloads per auth method. No auth: {"jsonData":{"auth_method":"none"}}. Basic auth: {"basicAuth":true,"basicAuthUser":"<user>","jsonData":{"auth_method":"basicAuth"},"secureJsonData":{"basicAuthPassword":"<pw>"}}. Digest auth: same as basic but auth_method='digestAuth'. Bearer token: {"jsonData":{"auth_method":"bearerToken"},"secureJsonData":{"bearerToken":"<token>"}}. API key: {"jsonData":{"auth_method":"apiKey","apiKeyKey":"X-API-Key","apiKeyType":"header"},"secureJsonData":{"apiKeyValue":"<val>"}} (apiKeyType='query' puts it in the URL query string instead). Forward OAuth: {"jsonData":{"auth_method":"oauthPassThru","oauthPassThru":true}}. AWS SigV4: {"jsonData":{"auth_method":"aws","aws":{"authType":"keys","region":"us-east-1","service":"monitoring"}},"secureJsonData":{"awsAccessKey":"<AKIA…>","awsSecretKey":"<secret>"}}. Azure Blob: {"jsonData":{"auth_method":"azureBlob","azureBlobCloudType":"AzureCloud","azureBlobAccountName":"<acct>"},"secureJsonData":{"azureBlobAccountKey":"<key>"}}.

OAuth2 payload recipes. Client credentials: {"jsonData":{"auth_method":"oauth2","oauth2":{"oauth2_type":"client_credentials","client_id":"<id>","token_url":"https://example.com/oauth/token","scopes":["read"],"authStyle":0}},"secureJsonData":{"oauth2ClientSecret":"<secret>"}}. JWT (service account): {"jsonData":{"auth_method":"oauth2","oauth2":{"oauth2_type":"jwt","email":"sa@project.iam.gserviceaccount.com","token_url":"https://oauth2.googleapis.com/token","scopes":["https://www.googleapis.com/auth/monitoring.read"]}},"secureJsonData":{"oauth2JWTPrivateKey":"<PEM>"}}. Missing oauth2_type is auto-filled to 'client_credentials' by the backend LoadSettings. The 'others' grant type is intentionally inert — it shows a discussion link in the editor and no OAuth is actually performed. Extra token-endpoint form fields go through jsonData.oauth2EndPointParamsName<N>/secureJsonData.oauth2EndPointParamsValue<N> pairs; extra headers sent WITH the token request go through jsonData.oauth2TokenHeadersName<N>/secureJsonData.oauth2TokenHeadersValue<N> pairs.

Legacy auth interpretation (pkg/models/settings.go:393-401): datasources created before jsonData.auth_method existed are back-filled at load time. If auth_method is empty and root.basicAuth is true, the effective method is 'basicAuth'. If auth_method is empty and jsonData.oauthPassThru is true, the effective method is 'oauthPassThru'. Otherwise empty auth_method is treated as 'none'. Do not assume a missing auth_method means the datasource is unconfigured.

secureJsonData values (basicAuthPassword, bearerToken, apiKeyValue, awsAccessKey, awsSecretKey, oauth2ClientSecret, oauth2JWTPrivateKey, azureBlobAccountKey, tlsCACert, tlsClientCert, tlsClientKey, proxyUserPassword, and every indexed httpHeaderValue<N>/secureQueryValue<N>/oauth2EndPointParamsValue<N>/oauth2TokenHeadersValue<N>) are write-only: they can be set or reset but never read back. To check whether a secret is already configured on an existing datasource, read the secureJsonFields object (e.g. secureJsonFields.bearerToken == true) instead of secureJsonData.

Connection, URL, and indexed-pair storage. root.url is the Base URL prefixed to every query URL and is optional — leave blank to fully specify the URL in each query. When the editor submits an empty URL the frontend stores the sentinel '__IGNORE_URL__' (src/constants.ts:72) and LoadSettings normalizes it back to '' (pkg/models/settings.go:296-298). When root.url is empty AND auth_method is not 'none'/'azureBlob' (or any TLS toggle, custom header, secure query param, or keepCookies entry is set), jsonData.allowedHosts MUST contain the target host or the datasource fails to load (pkg/models/settings.go:169-203); an 'Accept' or 'Content-Type' custom header does NOT force allowedHosts on its own (:184-190). jsonData.timeoutInSeconds defaults to 60 when unset and is clamped 0..300 by the editor; jsonData.unsecuredQueryHandling defaults to 'warn' when empty. Custom HTTP headers and URL query params are stored as INDEXED PAIRS (dynamic keys) and ARE now modeled as first-class indexedPair array fields — jsonData_httpHeaders (jsonData.httpHeaderName<N> / secureJsonData.httpHeaderValue<N>) and jsonData_secureQuery (jsonData.secureQueryName<N> / secureJsonData.secureQueryValue<N>), both in the urls-headers-params group; each editor row maps to one indexed pair (see src/components/config/SecureFieldsEditor.tsx:98-113). The OAuth2 endpoint params (jsonData.oauth2EndPointParamsName<N> / secureJsonData.oauth2EndPointParamsValue<N>) and OAuth2 token request headers (jsonData.oauth2TokenHeadersName<N> / secureJsonData.oauth2TokenHeadersValue<N>) are the same indexed-pair shape but remain NOT modeled as first-class fields here (oauth2-only; tracked as a follow-up). LoadSettings aggregates each set into a map (pkg/models/settings.go:389-392,427-443).
